lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: Well look who it is! The hero of Prifddinas!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do I feel like you're being sarcastic?

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: That's likely due to the fact that I am being sarcastic.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you're no hero either. You blindly served Lord Iorwerth right to the very end. You're lucky the other elves pardoned you.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: You seem upset by that. I served Lord Iorwerth because I didn't have a choice. The other elves realised that, hence the pardon.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: Yes, but you're a mercenary. You're lucky enough to be able to side with the highest bidder. Not all of us are so privileged.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: I was born into the Iorwerth clan, Lord Iorwerth was my uncle. Do you think I had a say in what my life would be? Do you think any of us did?

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: We served Lord Iorwerth because he was our leader. We may not have agreed with all that he did, but that didn't matter. He was in charge and we were not.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: Had I been born into the Cadarn clan, I'd be considered a hero right now. But I wasn't, so instead I am the villain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can make excuses, but Iestin served Lord Iorwerth and he switched sides.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: He did. Iestin was brave, I respect that. His bravery has rewarded him with leadership over our clan. But bravery comes easy when you have nothing left to lose.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: But I can see I will not change your mind. That's fine, I don't need to.

lines.speaker: Morvran
lines.text: That said, you'd do well to remember that the actions of Lord Iorwerth should not be used to judge our entire clan.
